metadata.name: py
metadata.description: Python compiler. Compiles Python source — typically a `def` followed by the function name — into a runtime Fn via Pyodide. Pyodide is dynamic-imported on first compile and the runtime instance is shared across all py-kind cels. v1 runs main-thread; worker isolation is v2.

Bootstrap switch for the py kind. true at boot — Pyodide lazy-loads on first py cel compile so apps that ship no py code pay nothing. Apps that want lazy install can flip this false before hydrate (forthcoming when ready-flip lifecycle is wired in v2).

Runtime-ready sentinel. v1 lazy-loads Pyodide on first compile (so this is observationally true once hydrate awaits the first py cel); v2 with a worker will flip it false→true at boot for accurate spinner UX. Defaults true.
Catastrophic-death sentinel. Main-thread Pyodide can't crash without taking the whole process down, so this stays true. Becomes meaningful when py moves into a worker.
Append-only log of CelError values produced by py lambdas. v1: empty; trap-as-value (see kernel/runCycle.ts) stores Python exceptions on the failing cel's v. Reserved for diagnostic accumulation.

When true, py-compiler spawns a Node worker_threads worker (or browser Worker) hosting Pyodide, and every compile/call becomes a postMessage round-trip returning a Promise. py.ready genuinely transitions false → true at boot. When false (default), Pyodide runs main-thread — sync after the first lazy-load. Tests that mock host fns rely on main-thread mode.

metadata.name: py-to-js
metadata.description: Bridge: convert a py-domain value (PyProxy or already-JS scalar) to a JS-domain value. Calls pyodide.toJs() on PyProxies; identity for already-converted scalars. Authors invoke via (py-to-js someCel).

metadata.name: js-to-py
metadata.description: Bridge: convert a JS-domain value to a py-domain value. Calls pyodide.toPy(). For scalars this is essentially identity (Python ints, floats, strs interoperate freely). For composites it builds a Python dict/list/etc. Authors invoke via (js-to-py someCel).
